design_decisions.summary: Temp directory lifecycle — download to os.MkdirTemp, feed to Vendor(), clean on return; no persistent cache.
Digest algorithm SHA-256 — crypto/sha256 stdlib, hex-encoded, stored as upstream-digest: sha256:<hex> in VERSION.
VERSION record written after successful Vendor — commit SHA + digest written only on nil error so a failed run leaves no stale pin.
Flag semantics — --upstream makes positional source-dir optional; --tag/--repo only apply with --upstream; no --tag uses pinned semver from baton.Version().
No SourceProvider interface — single FetchUpstream(ctx, repo, tag) (*FetchResult, error) function called from cmd/sworn/baton.go; no pipeline change.

reachability_artifacts: integration-test: TestBatonVendorUpstream_Success — drives cmdBatonVendor with --upstream --repo --tag against an httptest.Server (Rule 1 command-level)
integration-test: TestBatonVendorUpstream_DigestMismatch — tampered tarball fails closed at command level
integration-test: TestBatonVendorUpstream_LocalBackCompat — local vendor path unchanged

violations: Gate 6 — AC3 (no `--tag` uses pinned semver from VERSION; never `latest`/HEAD) is claimed delivered in proof.md "Delivered" and "AC3", with falsifiable "test asserts the requested URL carries the pinned tag". But no such test exists: all `TestBatonVendorUpstream_*` calls pass explicit `--tag`; no test exercises `cmdBatonVendor` with `--upstream` (no `--tag`) and asserts the codeload URL uses the pinned tag from `baton.Version()`. The fallback code in `cmdBatonVendor` and `Version()` exist, but the required falsifiable evidence at command level (Rule 1) is absent. Proof.md references `TestVersionIsSemverNotSha` (S49 leaf test) which does not cover the upstream URL construction.
